system_function: Fork preserves recomputable evidence boundaries for AI-assisted workflows by sealing declared artifacts, non-claims, unknown classes, and verification results.
pipeline_position: Downstream evidence-boundary preservation layer. Fork observes available workflow artifacts and preserves what can later be independently checked.

input_assumptions.statement: Upstream systems emit or export observable artifacts that Fork may preserve without controlling the source system.
Available artifacts may be incomplete, so source completeness must not be inferred.

supported_claims.statement: Fork can preserve declared artifacts in a bounded evidence record.
Fork can compute and report cryptographic hashes over preserved files.
Fork can report whether a preserved record verifies against its declared evidence boundary.

explicit_non_claims.statement: Fork does not claim AI output correctness.
Fork does not claim decision correctness.
Fork does not claim source completeness.
Fork does not claim legal admissibility.
Fork does not claim compliance satisfaction.
Fork does not claim audit sufficiency.
Fork does not claim institutional authority.
Fork does not claim runtime control.
Fork does not claim execution permissioning.
Fork does not claim policy authority.

emitted_artifacts.description: Machine-readable verification result with PASS, FAIL, or INDETERMINATE status.
Cross-environment normalized result excluding dynamic runtime metadata.
SHA-256 file manifest for preserved evidence artifacts.

consumable_artifacts.description: External runtime or anchoring receipt that may be preserved without inheriting correctness claims.
Available workflow event export or review artifact.

verification_model.verification_scope: Fork verifies declared artifact integrity and evidence-boundary consistency.
verification_model.verification_method: Strict schema-equivalent validation, deterministic file inspection, SHA-256 hashing, ID-reference checking, restricted-claim detection, manifest comparison, and explicit non-claim preservation.

evaluation_boundary.syntactic_integrity: Fork may verify that files, hashes, manifests, required fields, ID references, and declared boundary states are structurally present and consistent.
evaluation_boundary.semantic_validity: Semantic evaluation of whether preserved evidence supports a legal, compliance, audit, or safety conclusion belongs to external reviewers.
evaluation_boundary.evaluation_authority: External legal, compliance, audit, risk, or institutional reviewers retain evaluation authority.

authority_boundary.authority_basis: Fork is an evidence-boundary preservation system and does not carry institutional decision authority.
non_authorities: Fork does not approve or deny workflows.
Fork does not determine legal admissibility.
Fork does not accept institutional risk.
Fork does not perform runtime enforcement.

dependency_boundary.statement: Fork depends on available upstream artifacts and does not claim artifacts are complete.

declared_unknown_classes.statement: Unknown whether upstream systems emitted every relevant artifact.
Unknown whether external reviewers will accept preserved evidence as sufficient.

failure_states.statement: Missing required non-claims must fail mapping-record validation.
Unresolved dependencies may produce INDETERMINATE rather than PASS.
Unresolved safe-handoff ID references must fail mapping-record validation.

safe_handoffs.downstream_surface: Legal, compliance, audit, risk, or external review systems.

prohibited_claim_inheritance.statement: A Fork PASS result must not be inherited as AI output correctness.
A Fork PASS result must not be inherited as legal admissibility.
A Fork PASS result must not be inherited as compliance satisfaction.
A Fork PASS result must not be inherited as source completeness.

re_verification_requirements.statement: Any downstream use must verify file hashes and schema version before relying on the record.
Any downstream legal, compliance, or audit conclusion must be made by the appropriate authority, not inferred from Fork verification.
Any downstream use must verify safe-handoff ID references before attaching a new claim.

institutional_dependencies.statement: Legal, compliance, audit, and risk teams remain responsible for evaluating the meaning and sufficiency of preserved evidence.

mapping_summary: Fork is mapped as a recomputable evidence-boundary layer. It preserves artifacts, ID-linked non-claims, declared unknown classes, and re-verification duties, but does not inherit or create authority, correctness, compliance, audit, or legal conclusions.

declaration.claim_boundary_statement: Fork verifies declared evidence-boundary integrity only.
declaration.non_authority_statement: Fork does not possess legal, compliance, audit, policy, execution, or institutional authority.
declaration.handoff_statement: Fork handoffs must carry ID-referenced non-claims, unknown classes, prohibited inheritance rules, and re-verification requirements.
